name: A.I. Talk Story  |  Lt. Governor Candidate
description:
**Daniel Anthony** has become a legend of Hawai'i Agriculture, a partner\* with the **Hawaii Department of Agriculture** in his battle against the **Coconut Rhinoceros Beetle** and is now running for office.

This is a **Zoom** meeting.

He is serious about "**Making Hawai'i the first food secure state in America**". His plan for achieving that is cogent. All of us want Hawai'i to be more food secure (90% of food is currently imported, including drinking water). Mr Anthony also knows that **Hawaii needs A.I.**

We have direct evidence that **A.I. helps farmers in Hawai'i** with challenges like :
\* crop timing
\* legal assistance (i.e. lease agreements)
\* grant writing

We believe that A.I. can help them, tomorrow, with much more. What do you think? Come tell us.

name: Tech Meetup
description:
Low-key in-person meetup for people who are either in tech, interested in starting a career in tech, or love playing with tech as a hobby.

The meeting is at Whole Foods in Kaka'ako, in the outside cafe seating.

More info on the Honolulu Tech Network website:
[https://honolulu.network/~tech](https://honolulu.network/~tech/)

There's usually no real set topic, often people ask what we talk about so here's a sampling of common topics:

* AI
* ChatGPT
* Career stuff
* Other tech communities and events
* 3D printing
* VR/AR
* Web development
* Agile
* What places exist in Honolulu and where they are
* Renewable energy

name: A.I. Talk Story  |  "Field Notes Volume 01"
description:
O'ahu A.I. **Field Notes \| Volume 01**
**1**. What A.I. Is
**2**. A.I. Workbenches
**3**. A.I. Loops
**4**. Agent Orchestration
**5**. Knowledge Graphs

**Aloha** and welcome to the last half of 2026! A.I. development and disruption has only accelerated. In this meetup, Kevin will walk through the learnings that encompass the first O'ahu A.I. Publication: "**Field Notes Volume 01**". This booklet looks back at 3 years in the field of Applied A.I. engineering and product development, and how O'ahu and Hawai'i stand to benefit from our collective work.

6:00pm-6:30pm : Intros
**6:30pm-7:30pm** : Main event
7:30pm-8:30pm: Talk story

We will be at the **McCully–Mōʻiliʻili Public Library** at 2211 S King St, Honolulu, HI 96826 . There is a parking lot in the back, and we'll be right inside the entrance from it.

🌺 🌴 🌸

Also, **Hawaii Tech Week** is just around the corner! Visit *HawaiiTechWeek.com* and we hope to see you **Aug 31 - Sep 05 .**

name: Lawrence Edward Carter Sr.: Moral Courage and Nonviolent Transformation
description: Lawrence Edward Carter Sr.: Moral Courage and Nonviolent Transformation

name: Meet Hawaii's Champtionship Robotics Teams
description:
**We are excited to announce that this event will be running during Hawaii Tech Week .** Please see https://hawaiitechweek.com and sign up. **Mahalo!**

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-

Did you know that Hawai'i has a rich youth robotics scene?

When **Hawaiʻi’s robotics teams compete at the world championships**, they’re not just building robots—they’re building the skills our islands need. Join us for an afternoon **celebrating their achievements** and **connecting students, job seekers, career-changers, and local companies** investing in Hawaiʻi’s tech future. With robot demos!

\-\-\-

Our showcase features local robotics startups, teams from [Island Robotics ](https://www.friendsofislandrobotics.org/) and coaches like **Jared Haiola**, who has guided dozens of Hale'iwa students to VEX IQ World Championships over the past decade. His students don't just learn robotics—they learn to solve problems with limited resources, collaborate under pressure, and build things that work.

**Free Admission** *For Families & Individuals*
Bring your kids. Let them see what's possible when Hawai'i's youth get the right tools and mentorship. Let them talk to the teams. Let them imagine themselves building the technology our islands need.

**Become a Sponsor** *Reach out!*
Kevin@Oahu.AI

\-\-\-\-\-
*We will be checking registrations at the door. Keiki are welcome and do not need to be registered for the event. Organizers may refuse entry or remove attendees to maintain a safe environment. Mahalo for understanding.*
\-\-\-\-

*If you share your mobile number on an O'ahu A.I. event form, we may text you about event reminders and updates. Msg frequency varies. Msg and data rates may apply. Reply STOP to opt out. See www.oahu.ai/privacy*
